subjects.name: Power Electronics

units.title: Power Semi-Conductor Devices
topics: Silicon controlled rectifier (SCR): two transistor analogy, static and dynamic characteristics
Turn-on and turn-off methods; triggering methods (R, RC and UJT); snubber circuit design
Static and dynamic characteristics of Power MOSFET and Power IGBT
Numerical problems

units.title: Single-phase & Three-phase AC-DC Converters
topics: Single-phase half-wave controlled rectifiers with R and RL loads, with and without freewheeling diode
Single-phase fully controlled mid-point and bridge converters (R, RL, RLE); continuous/discontinuous conduction; effect of source inductance; semi-converter; dual converter
Three-phase half-wave, fully controlled and semi converters with R and RL loads; output voltage expressions
Numerical problems

units.title: AC-AC Converters
topics: Single-phase AC-AC power control by phase control with R and RL loads
Expression for rms output voltage
Single-phase step-down and step-up cycloconverters
Numerical problems

units.title: DC-DC Converters
topics: Operation of basic chopper; analysis of buck, boost and buck-boost converters in CCM and DCM
Output voltage equations using volt-sec balance in CCM and DCM
Output voltage ripple and inductor current ripple; control techniques; introduction to PWM control
Numerical problems

units.title: DC-AC Converters
topics: Single-phase half-bridge and full-bridge inverters with R and RL loads; phase displacement control
PWM with bipolar and unipolar voltage switching
Three-phase square wave inverters (120-degree and 180-degree conduction); sinusoidal PWM; current source inverter (CSI)
Numerical problems

seo.title: Power Electronics - JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Power Electronics: power semiconductor devices (SCR, MOSFET, IGBT), AC-DC/AC-AC/DC-DC converters and DC-AC inverters.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Professional Core. Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Digital Circuits

units.title: Combinational Logic Circuits - I
topics: Combinational logic definition, canonical forms, switching equations from truth tables
Simplification using Boolean theorems, NAND/NOR implementations, Karnaugh maps (3, 4, 5 variables), don't-care terms
Simplifying max-term equations, Quine-McCluskey minimization technique, general approach to combinational logic design
Look-ahead carry adder, cascading full adders, 4-bit adder-subtractor, BCD adder, Excess-3 adder, binary comparators

units.title: Combinational Logic Circuits - II
topics: Decoders, BCD decoders, 7-segment decoder, higher-order decoders
Multiplexers, higher-order multiplexing, de-multiplexers; realization of Boolean functions using decoders and multiplexers
Encoders, priority encoder
ROM and R/W memories, PROM, PAL, PLA basic structures, programming tables, realization of Boolean functions

units.title: Sequential Logic Circuits
topics: Flip-flop timing considerations, master-slave and edge-triggered flip-flops, characteristic equations
Flip-flops with reset and clear terminals, excitation tables, flip-flop conversion
Design of asynchronous and synchronous counters, modulus-N counters, Johnson counter, ring counter
Registers: buffer, control buffer, shift, bidirectional shift and universal shift registers

units.title: Sequential Circuit Design
topics: Mealy and Moore models, state machine notation
Synchronous sequential circuit analysis, construction of state diagrams
Analysis of clocked sequential circuits, realization of a sequence detector circuit
State reduction and assignments, design procedure

units.title: Digital Integrated Circuits
topics: Logic levels, propagation delay time, power dissipation, fan-out and fan-in, noise margin
Logic families: RTL and DTL circuits, TTL
Emitter-coupled logic (ECL), metal-oxide semiconductor (MOS)
Complementary MOS (CMOS), CMOS transmission gate circuits

seo.title: Digital Circuits - JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Digital Circuits: combinational logic, decoders/multiplexers/PLDs, sequential circuits, sequential circuit design and digital ICs.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Professional Core. Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and 5-unit syllabus read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Power Systems-II

units.title: Transmission Line Parameters Calculations
topics: Conductor materials and types; resistance for solid conductors
Inductance for single-phase and three-phase single and double circuit lines; concept of GMR and GMD
Symmetrical and asymmetrical configuration with and without transposition; bundled conductors; skin and proximity effects
Capacitance for 2-wire and 3-wire systems; effect of ground; capacitance for symmetrical/asymmetrical single and double circuit lines

units.title: Performance Analysis of Transmission Lines
topics: Classification: short, medium and long lines and their model representation
Nominal-T, nominal-pi and ABCD constants for symmetrical networks
Rigorous solution for long line equations; equivalent T and equivalent pi models
Surge impedance and surge impedance loading; regulation and efficiency; Ferranti effect

units.title: Power System Transients
topics: Types of system transients; propagation of surges; attenuation and distortion
Reflection and refraction coefficients
Termination of lines: open-circuited, short-circuited, through a resistance, connected to a cable
Reflection and refraction at a T-junction

units.title: Factors Governing Performance of Transmission Line
topics: Skin and proximity effects; Ferranti effect
Corona: description of the phenomenon, types of corona
Critical voltages and power loss; factors affecting corona; advantages and disadvantages
Radio interference

units.title: Sag and Tension Calculations & Overhead Line Insulators
topics: Sag and tension calculations with equal and unequal heights of towers; effect of wind and ice
Stringing chart and sag template and their applications
Types of insulators; voltage distribution in suspension insulators
String efficiency and methods for its improvement; capacitance grading and static shielding

seo.title: Power Systems-II - JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Power Systems-II: transmission line parameters, performance analysis, power system transients, corona and sag/tension with insulators.

subjects.name: Power Electronics Lab

seo.title: Power Electronics Lab - R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Power Electronics Lab: device characteristics, firing circuits, converters, cycloconverter, choppers and inverters.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Professional Core lab (0-0-3-1.5). Experiment list read from the source PDF: device characteristics, R/RC/UJT firing circuits, single-phase full/semi/dual and three-phase full converters, AC voltage regulator, cycloconverter, buck/boost converters, square-wave and SPWM inverters.

subjects.name: Analog and Digital Circuits Lab

seo.title: Analog & Digital Circuits Lab - R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Analog & Digital Circuits Lab: amplifiers, oscillators, 555/741/565 IC circuits, adders, multiplexers, flip-flops and counters.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Professional Core lab (0-0-3-1.5). Part A (analog): clipper/clamper, transistor biasing, feedback amplifiers, Wien-bridge and RC phase-shift oscillators, IC 741 integrator/differentiator, IC 555 multivibrators/Schmitt trigger, IC 565 PLL, ADC/DAC. Part B (digital): full adder/subtractor, IC 7483 adder, decoders, multiplexers, IC 7476 JK flip-flop, IC 7495 shift registers, ripple/synchronous/ring/Johnson counters. Read from the source PDF.

subjects.name: Soft Skills

units.title: Analytical Thinking & Listening Skills
topics: Self-introduction, self-analysis, developing a positive attitude, perception
Listening activity (Shaping Young Minds - a talk by Azim Premji)
Verbal communication
Non-verbal communication (body language)

units.title: Self-Management Skills & Etiquette
topics: Anger management, stress management, time management
Six thinking hats, team building, leadership qualities
Social and business etiquette

topics: Basic grammar, tenses, prepositions, pronunciation
Letter writing

Email and letter writing

units.title: Job-Oriented Skills
topics: Group discussion and mock group discussions
Resume preparation
Interview skills
Mock interview

units.title: Interpersonal Relationships
topics: Introduction, importance, types and uses
Factors affecting interpersonal relationships
Accommodating different styles
Consequences of interpersonal relationships

seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Soft Skills: analytical thinking, listening, self-management, communication, job-oriented skills and interpersonal relationships.

subjects.name: Tinkering Lab

seo.title: Tinkering Lab - JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-1 Tinkering Lab (engineering science): breadboard circuits, LDR street light, Arduino and ESP32 projects, sensor interfacing and 3D printing.

source.college_source_note: Both the subject list and detailed unit topics are sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus and have not been independently cross-confirmed against JNTUK's own exam records -- autonomous colleges may adapt content locally.

subjects.notes: Engineering Science lab (0-0-2-1). Experiment list read from the source PDF: breadboard series/parallel circuits, traffic-light circuit, automatic street light using LDR, Arduino LED blinking (Tinkercad and IDE), IR sensor and servo motor interfacing, ESP32 LED/LDR, mobile-app LED control, 3D-printed walking robot and rocket, soil-moisture monitoring, and a design-thinking motorbike redesign.

subjects.name: Electrical Measurements and Instrumentation

units.title: Analog Ammeters and Voltmeters
topics: Classification; deflecting, control and damping torques
PMMC, moving iron type and electrostatic instruments: construction, torque equation, range extension
Errors and compensations; advantages and disadvantages
Instrument transformers: CT and PT theory, ratio and phase angle errors; numerical problems

units.title: Analog Wattmeters and Power Factor Meters
topics: Electrodynamometer type wattmeter (LPF and UPF)
Power factor meters: dynamometer and MI type (single and three phase), construction and torque equation
Potentiometers: DC Crompton's potentiometer, standardization and applications
AC potentiometer (polar and coordinate types), standardization and applications; numerical problems

units.title: Measurement of Electrical Parameters
topics: DC bridges: Wheatstone's bridge (medium resistance), Kelvin's double bridge (low resistance)
Loss of charge method (high resistance), Megger, measurement of earth resistance
AC bridges for inductance and Q: Maxwell's, Hay's and Anderson's bridges
AC bridges for capacitance and loss angle: De Sauty's, Schering and Wien's bridges; numerical problems

units.title: Transducers
topics: Definition and classification
Resistive, inductive and capacitive transducers; LVDT; strain gauge
Thermistors, thermocouples, piezoelectric and photodiode transducers
Hall effect sensors; numerical problems

units.title: Digital Meters
topics: Digital voltmeters: successive approximation, ramp type and integrating type DVM
Digital frequency meter, digital multimeter, digital tachometer
Digital energy meter, Q meter
CRO: measurement of phase difference and frequency using Lissajous patterns; numerical problems

seo.title: Electrical Measurements & Instrumentation - EEE 3-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-2 Electrical Measurements & Instrumentation: analog meters, wattmeters, PF meters, DC/AC bridges, transducers and digital meters.

subjects.name: Microprocessors and Microcontrollers

units.title: Introduction to Microprocessor Architecture
topics: Introduction and evolution of microprocessors
Architecture of 8086
Memory and register organization of 8086
Introduction to 80286, 80386, 80486 and Pentium (architectural advancements only)

units.title: Minimum and Maximum Mode Operations
topics: Instruction sets of 8086, addressing modes, assembler directives, simple programs
General bus operation of 8086
Minimum and maximum mode operations of 8086; control signal interfacing
Read and write cycle timing diagrams

units.title: Microprocessor I/O Interfacing
topics: 8255 PPI: architecture and modes of operation; interfacing I/O devices to 8086 using 8255
Interfacing ADC and DAC converters
Stepper motor interfacing; static memory interfacing with 8086
Architecture and interfacing of DMA controller (8257)

units.title: 8051 Microcontroller
topics: Overview and architecture of 8051; memory organization and register set
Instruction set and simple programs
I/O ports and interrupts; timers and counters
Serial communication; interfacing of peripherals

units.title: PIC Architecture
topics: Block diagram of the basic PIC 18 microcontroller
Registers and I/O ports
Programming in C for PIC: data types, I/O programming
Logical operations and data conversion

seo.title: Microprocessors & Microcontrollers - R23 EEE 3-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-2 Microprocessors & Microcontrollers: 8086 architecture, min/max mode operation, I/O interfacing (8255/8257), 8051 microcontroller and PIC 18.

subjects.name: Power System Analysis

units.title: Circuit Topology & Per Unit Representation
topics: Graph theory definitions; element-node and bus incidence matrices; primitive network representation
Formation of Ybus matrix by singular transformation and direct inspection methods
Per unit quantities; single line diagram; impedance diagram of a power system
Numerical problems

units.title: Power Flow Studies
topics: Necessity of power flow studies; derivation of static power flow equations
Power flow solution using Gauss-Seidel method
Newton-Raphson method (rectangular and polar coordinate forms)
Decoupled and fast decoupled methods; algorithmic approach; numerical problems on a 3-bus system

units.title: Z-Bus Algorithm & Symmetrical Fault Analysis
topics: Formation of Zbus; algorithm for modification of Zbus matrix (without mutual impedance)
Reactances of synchronous machine
Three-phase short circuit currents; short circuit MVA calculations
Numerical problems

units.title: Symmetrical Components & Unsymmetrical Fault Analysis
topics: Definition and symmetrical components of unbalanced three-phase systems; power in symmetrical components
Sequence impedances and sequence networks of synchronous generator, transformers and transmission line
Unsymmetrical faults: LG, LL, LLG and LLL on an unloaded alternator
Numerical problems

units.title: Power System Stability Analysis
topics: Elementary concepts of steady state, dynamic and transient stabilities; swing equation
Steady state stability
Equal area criterion of stability and its applications
Factors affecting transient stability; methods to improve steady state and transient stability; numerical problems

seo.title: Power System Analysis - JNTUK R23 EEE 3-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 EEE 3-2 Power System Analysis: Ybus and per-unit, power flow studies, Zbus and faults, symmetrical components and stability.
